meta.author: Blueprint Agent (normalised from founder-authored source document)

business.name: Phoenix Automation
business.tagline: Transform Your Business With Intelligent Automation
business.description: Phoenix Automation helps businesses eliminate manual processes by identifying operational inefficiencies and implementing AI-powered workflows that run automatically. The model is simple: free assessment to earn trust, paid build to deliver value, monthly retainer to sustain the relationship. The agency runs on a minimal team with Claude and n8n as the operational core. Infrastructure is fully live as of April 2026 — Airtable, ClickUp, n8n Cloud, and all intake tooling are operational and the agency is approaching its first paying client.

target_segments.name: E-commerce Brands
target_segments.description: Online retailers drowning in manual order management, inventory updates, customer comms, and fulfilment ops that consume hours daily.
target_segments.estimated_size: Large — millions of SMB e-commerce operators globally

target_segments.name: Professional Services
target_segments.description: Law firms, financial advisors, and consultancies paying staff to handle intake, document processing, reporting, and scheduling that can be automated.
target_segments.estimated_size: Large — hundreds of thousands of professional services SMBs in the US alone

target_segments.name: Healthcare Admin
target_segments.description: Healthcare practices and admin teams with data management overhead — appointment scheduling, patient comms, billing reconciliation, and report generation.
target_segments.estimated_size: Large — underserved by AI tooling due to complexity

target_segments.name: SMBs with Repetitive Manual Ops
target_segments.description: Any small-to-medium business paying people to do repetitive tasks — data entry, copy-paste between tools, report generation, follow-up sequences — that should be automated.

icp.description: Operations managers, founders, and business owners at SMBs who are actively paying staff to do repetitive manual tasks and know it is a problem. They are time-poor, growth-constrained by operational overhead, and open to technology — but not developers themselves.
icp.role: Operations Manager, Founder, or Business Owner
trigger_events: Hiring more staff to handle volume that should be automated
Manually copying data between platforms every day
Losing 10+ hours per week to repetitive admin that creates no strategic value
Growth stalling because operations cannot scale with the team size
Recent failed attempt to use off-the-shelf tools that didn't fit their workflow
disqualifiers: Fewer than 10 employees — ROI on automation is too thin
Unwilling to own their own API accounts and pay their own usage costs
Expects AI to fully replace human judgment with no oversight
Highly regulated environments requiring certified compliance (e.g. HIPAA-covered entities without legal counsel)

pain_points: Paying staff to do repetitive tasks that take hours but create no strategic value
Manual data entry between disconnected tools causing errors and delays
No operational visibility without pulling manual reports
Unable to scale revenue without proportionally growing headcount
Spending 3–5 hours per week on follow-ups, status updates, and scheduling that could be automated

competitors.name: Freelance n8n / Zapier builders
competitors.differentiator: Phoenix Automation uses Claude Code + n8n-MCP to build in under 1 hour vs. 3–5 hours manually — delivering faster, at lower cost, with self-healing workflows

competitors.name: Traditional software agencies
competitors.differentiator: No custom code, no lock-in — clients own all credentials and workflow docs. Faster delivery, lower price, async-first model

competitors.name: Off-the-shelf automation tools (Zapier, Make)
competitors.differentiator: Done-for-you implementation with Claude AI baked in — not a tool the client has to learn and maintain themselves

services.name: Free Business Assessment
services.description: A 30-minute consultation that identifies 3–5 automation opportunities in the client's business and calculates rough ROI. This is the sales entry point — it doubles as the trust-builder and the sales call. Every assessment ends with a proposal outline.
inputs: Typeform pre-assessment (industry, team size, biggest operational pain, hours lost per week to manual tasks)
outputs: 3–5 identified automation opportunities
Rough ROI calculation
Proposal outline presented on the call

services.name: Starter Build
services.description: 1–2 automations built and deployed using Claude Code + n8n-MCP. Designed for first-time clients testing AI-powered automation before committing to a larger engagement. Example: lead qualifier + CRM auto-update.
inputs: Client API credentials via 1Password or encrypted form
Access to client's n8n workspace (own project folder)
Defined workflow requirements from assessment call
outputs: 1–2 live, tested automated workflows in client's n8n instance
Loom video walkthrough (async — no live training call)
ClickUp project documentation (shared read-only link)

services.name: Growth Package
services.description: 3–5 workflow automations with custom Claude AI integrations, full deployment, and async training via Loom. The most common engagement for ops-heavy SMBs who are ready to automate at scale. Typically includes the client's highest-volume manual processes.
inputs: Client API credentials for all tools being automated
Full access to client's tool stack (CRM, comms, data sources)
Stakeholder interview notes from assessment call
outputs: 3–5 live, tested automated workflows
Custom Claude AI integrations where applicable
Loom training library (one video per workflow)
ClickUp project documentation with workflow logic explained

services.name: Agency Retainer
services.description: Monthly monitoring, bug fixes, new automations, and Claude-generated performance reports. n8n monitors automations and alerts on failures. Claude auto-generates a monthly performance report from n8n execution data. The recurring revenue backbone of the agency.
inputs: Existing live automations in client's n8n instance
outputs: Monthly performance report (Claude-written, data-backed)
Bug fixes and maintenance within agreed SLA
n8n monitoring and automated failure alerts
New automation additions as included in tier

value_proposition.headline: We replace your manual operations with AI workflows in under a week — starting with a free assessment that always ends with a concrete ROI estimate.
differentiators: Claude Code + n8n-MCP builds client automations in 30–60 minutes vs. 3–5 hours manually — enabling 4–6× more client capacity without adding headcount
Free assessment IS the sales call — removes purchase risk and pre-qualifies ROI before the client spends a dollar
Clients own all their API credentials and workflow documentation — clean break with no vendor lock-in if they ever leave
Fully async delivery model (proposals, training, status updates all automated) — premium experience with minimal founder hours per client
Self-healing automations: Claude Code detects and self-corrects n8n errors automatically, reducing support burden
proof_points: [TO BE CONFIRMED] — First client case studies in progress. Replace after first 1–2 deliveries.
Efficiency benchmark: 3–5 hrs/workflow manual → 30–60 min with Claude Code + n8n-MCP (4–6× improvement)
value_proposition.positioning_statement: For SMB operations managers and founders who are paying people to do repetitive tasks, Phoenix Automation provides done-for-you AI workflow automation — unlike freelancers who build and disappear or software agencies that lock you into custom code you can't own.

databases: Airtable (lead and client database — 50+ fields in Clients table, single source of truth for the agency)
integrations: n8n Cloud (kaiashley.app.n8n.cloud — automation backbone, all 6 credentials active)
Apollo.io (lead prospecting by industry, company size, job title)
Instantly.ai (personalised cold email sequences, inbox health management — pending setup)
Loom (async client training — replaces live walkthrough calls)
LinkedIn Sales Navigator (decision-maker filtering for outreach)
Meta Ads (free automation audit lead magnet)
1Password (secure credential handoff from client to agency)
tech_stack.hosting: n8n Cloud (kaiashley.app.n8n.cloud). Client automations run in client's own n8n account — each client has a separate n8n account (Option A model).
client_facing_tools: ClickUp (shared read-only project links — replaces client portal until 5+ clients)
Loom (async video walkthroughs — no live training sessions)

steps: Client discovers Phoenix Automation via ad, referral, LinkedIn, or website organic
Client clicks CTA ('Get Free Assessment') and is directed to Typeform intake form (https://form.typeform.com/to/RSsWJkcf)
Client fills 5-question Typeform: industry, team size, biggest operational pain, hours lost per week to manual tasks — data auto-posts to Airtable via n8n
Claude pre-scores the lead automatically from Typeform data and updates Airtable record
Client books 30-minute assessment call via Calendly (https://calendly.com/kai-phoenixautomation/free-business-assessment)
Owner conducts assessment call on Zoom/Meet: identifies 3–5 automation opportunities, calculates rough ROI — this IS the sales call, always ends with a proposal outline
Owner reviews Claude-drafted proposal (from call notes) within 24 hours and sends to client
Client signs agreement and pays — onboarding checklist triggers automatically via n8n + ClickUp, including API key collection request

delivery_workflow.methodology: Blueprint-first, async delivery — no live check-ins, no manual status updates

phases.name: Onboarding
phases.description: Client signs and pays. ClickUp shared project link sent automatically. n8n onboarding automation triggers: API key collection form (1Password or encrypted), client n8n workspace created, credentials template populated with pre-authenticated nodes. Zero risk of credentials crossing between projects.

phases.name: Build
phases.description: Owner describes workflow to Claude Code in plain English. Claude Code plans the n8n workflow, n8n-MCP builds it node by node via API (access to 1,084+ node schemas), Claude Code tests each node immediately and self-corrects on errors. Owner reviews the completed, tested workflow and activates it. Client receives weekly auto-status updates from ClickUp — no manual check-ins.

phases.name: Launch & Handoff
phases.description: Automations go live. Client receives short Loom video walkthroughs (one per workflow) — no live training sessions needed. Client confirms everything works as expected. Referral trigger sequence queued in n8n for 30 days post-launch.

phases.name: Retainer
phases.description: Client moves to monthly retainer. n8n monitors automations and alerts on failures automatically. Claude auto-generates a monthly performance report from n8n execution data. Owner handles relationship management, upsells, and complex architecture decisions only.

communication_channels: ClickUp (weekly automated status updates — no manual emails)
Loom (async video walkthroughs for training and handoff)
Email via Instantly.ai (Claude-written cold outreach and follow-up sequences)
operations.project_management: ClickUp — one Space per client, tracks every task from assessment to retainer. Shared read-only links replace the need for a custom client portal initially.
sops: Credentials handoff: client creates API key → sends via 1Password or encrypted form → added to n8n client folder only → Claude Code references template → live automation billed to client
Assessment call framework: identify 3–5 automatable processes, calculate rough ROI, always close with proposal outline — never deliver assessment without next steps
Proposal rule: every proposal states clearly that client pays their own API costs ($50–$300/mo typical) — no surprises
Referral SOP: automated email sequence triggered 30 days post-launch via n8n — never ask for referrals manually

AI boundary rule: Claude handles cold outreach, lead scoring, proposal drafting, status updates, reports — human handles assessment calls, final proposal review, complex architecture, and relationship management

tiers.name: Free Assessment
tiers.description: 30-minute consultation identifying automation opportunities and calculating ROI. The sales entry point — always ends with a proposal. Never delivered without presenting next steps.
inclusions: Custom proposal outline presented on the call

tiers.name: Starter Build
tiers.description: 1–2 automations built and deployed. Entry-level paid engagement for first-time clients testing AI before committing to a larger package.
inclusions: 1–2 live automated workflows in client's n8n instance
Claude Code + n8n-MCP build (30–60 min per workflow)
Loom video walkthrough (async — no live training)
exclusions: Ongoing monitoring and bug fixes (add Agency Retainer)

tiers.name: Growth Package
tiers.description: 3–5 workflow automations with custom Claude AI integrations, full deployment, and async Loom training. The most common engagement. Targets clients ready to automate at scale.
inclusions: Custom Claude AI integrations where applicable
Loom training library (one video per workflow)
ClickUp project documentation with workflow logic
tiers.target_segment: Ops-heavy SMBs ready to automate multiple processes

tiers.name: Agency Retainer
tiers.description: Monthly monitoring, bug fixes, new automations, and Claude-generated performance reports. The recurring revenue backbone — converts one-time builds into ongoing relationships.
inclusions: n8n monitoring with automated failure alerts
Monthly performance report (Claude-generated from n8n data)
New automation additions (scope defined per tier level)
tiers.target_segment: Existing build clients moving to ongoing partnership

pricing.notes: The free assessment IS the sales call — never deliver one without presenting a proposal and next steps. Clients pay their own API usage costs ($50–$300/mo typical) — state this in every proposal. Agency fee covers build, deploy, and maintain only. Think contractor model: agency brings tools and expertise, client pays for materials (API accounts).

roles.title: Founder / Agency Owner
responsibilities: Assessment calls and sales (30 min per prospect — only human touchpoint in acquisition)
Final proposal review before sending (Claude drafts, owner reviews)
Oversight and sign-off on all delivered automations

roles.title: VA / Workflow Builder
responsibilities: Builds n8n workflows under Kai's direction and specifications
Executes workflow builds for client deliveries
Supports agency internal workflow development and testing

hiring_plan.title: Outreach VA
hiring_plan.trigger: When outbound volume and Apollo list management exceed founder's available time (approx. 8+ active outreach campaigns)

hiring_plan.title: Delivery VA
hiring_plan.trigger: When active client count exceeds 5 concurrent projects and simpler n8n workflows need building

hiring_plan.title: Support VA
hiring_plan.trigger: When client portal launches (5+ clients) and support ticket volume requires dedicated response time

cost_structure: Claude Code — pay-per-use Anthropic API (primary build tool, fully operational)
Instantly.ai — subscription for cold email sequences
Airtable — free tier (50+ field Clients base live)
GitHub — free tier (source control for agent definitions and workflows)

agent_map.name: Lead Qualifier Chatbot
agent_map.role: Qualifies inbound website visitors 24/7 using Claude API, asks 3 screening questions, routes hot leads to Calendly and cold leads to a nurture response.
triggers: Visitor clicks chat bubble on Phoenix Automation website
inputs: Visitor's answers to 3 qualification questions (industry, team size, biggest pain)
outputs: Qualification verdict (hot / cold)
Calendly booking link for hot leads
Nurture message for cold leads

agent_map.name: Outreach Agent
agent_map.role: Writes personalised cold outreach emails from Apollo prospect data and queues them in Instantly for automated sending — targeting 30–50 new contacts per day.
triggers: New prospect added to Airtable from Apollo export
outputs: Personalised cold email (Claude-written)
3-step follow-up sequence queued in Instantly

agent_map.name: Lead Scorer / Typeform Qualification
agent_map.role: Pre-scores inbound leads from Typeform submissions before the assessment call, updating Airtable with a qualification score and briefing notes for the owner.
triggers: New Typeform submission received (webhook via n8n — webhook registered and active)
inputs: Hours lost per week to manual tasks
outputs: Lead score (high / medium / low)
Pre-call briefing summary for owner
Airtable record updated

agent_map.name: Proposal Drafting Agent
agent_map.role: Drafts a custom proposal document within 24 hours of an assessment call using owner's call notes — including automations identified, timeline, cost estimate, and expected ROI.
triggers: Owner pastes assessment call notes into Claude Code or triggers via n8n
outputs: Custom proposal draft (Notion document): automations, timeline, cost, expected ROI

agent_map.name: Onboarding Automation
agent_map.role: Triggers the full client onboarding sequence when payment is confirmed — creates ClickUp project, sends welcome message, and initiates API key collection.
triggers: Client payment confirmed (payment platform webhook via n8n)
outputs: ClickUp project created with template tasks
Welcome email with ClickUp shared link sent to client
API key collection form triggered (1Password or encrypted form)
Client n8n workspace and credentials template created

agent_map.name: Build Agent (Claude Code + n8n-MCP)
agent_map.role: Builds, tests, and self-corrects n8n automation workflows from plain English descriptions using the n8n-MCP bridge — reducing build time from 3–5 hours to 30–60 minutes per workflow.
triggers: Owner describes workflow to Claude Code in plain English
inputs: Client credentials template in n8n (per-client folder)
n8n-skills companion repo for expert node knowledge
outputs: Live, tested n8n workflow built node by node via n8n API
Self-corrected on any errors before presenting to owner
Owner review and final activation

agent_map.name: Status Update Agent
agent_map.role: Sends automated weekly project status updates to clients from ClickUp task data — eliminating the need for manual check-in emails.
triggers: Weekly n8n cron schedule (Monday morning client timezone)
inputs: ClickUp project data (tasks completed, in progress, next milestone)
outputs: Weekly status email to client with project progress and next steps

agent_map.name: Reporting Agent
agent_map.role: Auto-generates monthly performance reports for retainer clients from n8n workflow execution data — showing automations run, errors caught, time saved, and value delivered.
triggers: Monthly n8n cron schedule (1st of each month)
inputs: n8n workflow execution logs for client
outputs: Monthly performance report (Claude-written, data-backed)
Email to client with report attached or linked

agent_map.name: Referral Trigger Agent
agent_map.role: Sends an automated referral request email sequence 30 days after client launch — systematically converting happy clients into referral sources without manual outreach.
triggers: 30 days after client project launch date (n8n scheduled trigger)
outputs: Referral request email sequence (Claude-written, 2–3 touch points)

planned_builds.name: Client Portal (Phase 2)
planned_builds.rationale: ClickUp shared links are sufficient until 5+ active clients. Build on Lovable or Bubble with Airtable API backend. Must-haves: secure login, onboarding checklist, project tracker, deliverables library, support tickets, invoice history, monthly report.

planned_builds.name: ClickUp Sync Workflow
planned_builds.rationale: Built in n8n (18 nodes, inactive) — syncs project data between internal ClickUp and client-facing views. Needs activation and testing before first client onboards.

planned_builds.name: Credential Follow-Up Workflow
planned_builds.rationale: Built in n8n (11 nodes, inactive) — sends automated follow-up when client has not submitted API credentials within a defined window. Kai activates before first client onboard.

operational.metric: Prospects contacted per day via automated outreach
Proposal turnaround time from assessment call

client_success.metric: Hours saved per client per week

client_success.target: 1 referral per client within 60 days of launch

assumptions.assumption: The free assessment + same-call proposal model will convert at > 30% to paid builds
assumptions.risk_if_wrong: Revenue pipeline collapses — all effort on assessments yields no revenue and burns founder time
assumptions.validation_method: Track assessment-to-proposal and proposal-to-paid conversion rates over the first 10 assessments; adjust offer if rate is below 20%

assumptions.assumption: Claude Code + n8n-MCP reliably builds production-grade client workflows in under 1 hour
assumptions.risk_if_wrong: Build times stay at 3–5 hours — the core operational advantage disappears and pricing model breaks
assumptions.validation_method: System is operational and in use for internal builds. Validate against client-grade workflow complexity before signing first client.

assumptions.assumption: SMB clients will accept the credential ownership model without friction during onboarding
assumptions.risk_if_wrong: Clients object to managing their own API accounts — onboarding drags and trust breaks early
assumptions.validation_method: Include the credential handoff script in the first 3 proposals and track objection rate; prepare a simplified 15-minute guided setup if needed

assumptions.assumption: 30–50 automated cold outreach contacts per day will generate sufficient assessment bookings to close 2+ clients per month
assumptions.risk_if_wrong: Pipeline is too thin — monthly revenue targets miss and the agency burns months in low-revenue limbo
assumptions.validation_method: Measure booking rate per 100 contacts over the first 30 days of outreach; if < 2%, adjust channel mix (LinkedIn DMs, Meta Ads) or ICP targeting

assumptions.assumption: Async delivery (Loom videos, no live training sessions) will be accepted as sufficient by clients paying $1,500–$7,000
assumptions.risk_if_wrong: Clients demand live calls — adds founder hours, caps capacity, and breaks the async-first model
assumptions.validation_method: Track requests for live calls in the first 5 projects; if > 40% request them, introduce a paid live walkthrough add-on rather than including it free

gaps.gap: Outreach Agent not yet built — Instantly.ai setup pending, blocking the outbound email pipeline
gaps.resolution: Complete Instantly.ai account setup (inbox warming, domain authentication), then build and activate the outreach n8n workflow. This is the primary revenue driver before referrals kick in.

gaps.gap: Website chatbot not yet embedded — inbound lead qualification from website visitors is non-functional
gaps.resolution: Connect chat widget to Claude API with a 3-question qualification prompt (industry, team size, biggest pain). Route hot leads to Calendly, cold leads to nurture. The Typeform form is live but the chatbot covers different traffic patterns.

gaps.gap: No real client case studies — website's 'Real Results' section shows placeholder content
gaps.resolution: Replace placeholder case studies after first 1–2 client deliveries. In the interim, the efficiency benchmark (3–5 hrs → 30–60 min) is a concrete proof point to use.

gaps.gap: Typeform intake form not yet embedded on website — form is live but not yet placed in the conversion flow
gaps.resolution: Embed https://form.typeform.com/to/RSsWJkcf before the Calendly booking step on the website. The form exists and is live — this is a website placement task only.

gaps.gap: MRR targets (3-month, 12-month) not formally defined — makes financial planning and decision-making inconsistent
gaps.resolution: Define after closing the first 2 clients. Use retainer conversion rate from early projects to project realistic MRR curve.

gaps.gap: No pricing anchor on website — visitors cannot gauge affordability before booking
gaps.resolution: Add 'Projects start from $1,500' line or a 3-tier pricing card to the website. Quick win — reduces the largest pre-call objection.

gaps.gap: No defined scope limit on Agency Retainer — risk of scope creep once clients move to monthly
gaps.resolution: Define maximum number of new automations and support hours included per retainer tier before signing the first retainer client.

gaps.gap: Privacy Policy and Terms of Service pages on website are empty
gaps.resolution: Draft and publish both pages before acquiring paying clients. Use a privacy policy generator for the first version.

gaps.gap: Credential Follow-Up and Typeform Lead Qualification workflows built but inactive — Kai must activate before first client onboards
gaps.resolution: Activate [PA] Credential Follow-Up (11 nodes) and confirm [PA] Typeform Lead Qualification (13 nodes) webhook is responding correctly. Both are built and tested.

gaps.gap: No defined process for handling client automation failures outside business hours
gaps.resolution: Define SLA for retainer clients (e.g. 24-hour response for non-critical failures, 4-hour for revenue-impacting). Include in retainer agreement template.

gaps.gap: EIN pending — business registration in Florida is in progress
gaps.resolution: Monitor EIN application. Required before issuing invoices to US clients or opening a business bank account.

build_priority.item: Activate Typeform Lead Qualification and Credential Follow-Up workflows
build_priority.rationale: Both workflows are built and tested. Activating them before the first client onboards ensures the intake and credential collection sequences fire automatically — zero manual intervention needed from day one.

build_priority.item: Embed Typeform intake form on website before Calendly step
build_priority.rationale: The Typeform form is live but not yet in the conversion flow on the website. Without this, leads book assessment calls unqualified and the lead-scorer agent receives no data to work with.

build_priority.item: Complete Instantly.ai setup and build Outreach Agent
build_priority.rationale: Outbound is the primary revenue driver before referrals are established. Instantly.ai inbox warming and domain authentication must be completed first, then the outreach n8n workflow activated. Target 30 contacts/day.
depends_on: Activate Typeform Lead Qualification and Credential Follow-Up workflows

build_priority.item: Close first real client and deliver first paid build
build_priority.rationale: First client is the validation milestone that proves the assessment-to-delivery model works end to end, generates initial revenue, and produces a real case study. Use personal network and early outreach pipeline. Infrastructure is ready.
depends_on: Activate Typeform Lead Qualification and Credential Follow-Up workflows

build_priority.item: Replace website placeholder case studies with first real client outcomes
build_priority.rationale: Social proof is the strongest conversion lever on the website. Cannot be completed until first client delivery is done — but should be executed immediately afterwards.
depends_on: Close first real client and deliver first paid build

build_priority.item: Activate lead qualifier chatbot on website
build_priority.rationale: Website chatbot qualifies inbound visitors 24/7 independently of the Typeform flow. Captures visitors who do not click the primary CTA. Claude API + 3-question prompt.

build_priority.item: Build error handling and alerting workflow in n8n
build_priority.rationale: As live workflows accumulate for real clients, automated error detection and alerting becomes critical for retainer SLA delivery. Should be built before the second client onboards.
depends_on: Close first real client and deliver first paid build

build_priority.item: Define and publish Agency Retainer scope limits and SLA
build_priority.rationale: Prevents scope creep once the first client transitions to retainer. Scope definition must exist before the retainer agreement is signed.
depends_on: Close first real client and deliver first paid build

build_priority.item: Build client portal (Lovable or Bubble + Airtable API)
build_priority.rationale: ClickUp shared links are sufficient until 5+ active clients. Build the portal only once delivery is proven and client volume justifies the investment.
depends_on: Close first real client and deliver first paid build
